Election.title: BV2132 — Pet poll: four methods, three winners
Election.description: One electorate, three pets (Dog, Cat, Fish), tallied four ways in a single election. Cat is the Condorcet winner (beats Dog 13-9 and Fish 15-7) and wins Approval and STAR — but has the fewest first choices, so choose-one Plurality elects the front-runner Dog and RCV-IRV eliminates Cat first and elects Fish. Result: Plurality→Dog, IRV→Fish, Approval→Cat, STAR→Cat. A made-up, small answer to meta_pets, tuned so the methods actually disagree.

logs: Dog has the most votes and wins the round
Cat has the most approvals and wins the round
